workstream: C — real Max-Q (300W) vs Workstation (600W), same SKU family, both sm_120 / 96GB
method: identical 200-step Arm-1 resident slice; config body sha256 db030f4c0eeab843 IDENTICAL on both (only output_dir/wandb_name differ); seed 42; same data order; axolotl fd12f92 + bnb e7f4d86 (sm_120, cu130) verified by SHA on both pods
gate2_pl_writable: no (host-locked, no SYS_ADMIN) — the handoff's -pl simulation was impossible, so BOTH halves are REAL silicon
throughput_definition: tokens/step = sequence_len(2048) x micro_batch(1) x grad_accum(4) = 8192 nominal packed tokens; applied IDENTICALLY to both halves. NOTE: an earlier 600W artifact recorded 1434 tok/s under a different, unreproducible definition (~4503 tok/step); it is superseded here rather than mixed.
s_per_step_method: median of steady-state progress-bar s/it, first 10 samples (warmup) dropped

workstation_600w.power_headroom_note: drew 483.9W median against a 600W cap — this workload never saturates the 600W budget

maxq_300w.sku: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Max-Q Workstation Edition

verdict: Max-Q delivers ~82% of the 600W card's training throughput at ~62% of its actual power draw -> ~+32% perf/W. Both cards hold the full 96GB and identical numerics (same sm_120 kernels).
caveats: DIFFERENT PHYSICAL HOSTS and clouds (600W=SECURE, Max-Q=COMMUNITY; Max-Q never appeared in SECURE across 8 attempts). Host CPU/PCIe/neighbour variance is NOT controlled. This is a cross-host cross-SKU comparison, stated as such.
single slice per SKU, no repeats; s/step medians are steady-state but each pod saw occasional shared-host stalls (excluded by the median, visible in the raw csv)
Max-Q sustained exactly its 300.0W cap (max 309.4W) -> it IS power-limited; the 600W card was NOT (483.9W of 600W). A more power-hungry workload would widen the gap.
